channel.title: AI News — A Guide to Cloud & AI
channel.description: Daily AI news digest — curated headlines, summaries and trends

item.title: Supercharge your AI agents: The New ADK Integrations Ecosystem
item.description: The Agent Development Kit (ADK) now features a new ecosystem of third-party integrations, allowing developers to connect AI agents with tools like GitHub and Notion. This expansion enhances the capabilities of AI applications in real-world scenarios.

item.title: How we built the Google I/O 2026 Save the Date experience
item.description: Google I/O 2026 is set for May 19-20, featuring a save the date puzzle that showcases AI's role in enhancing event experiences. This tradition engages attendees ahead of the main event.

item.title: You can't stream the energy: A developer's guide to Google Cloud Next '26 in Vegas
item.description: Google Cloud Next '26 in Las Vegas will focus on networking and hands-on problem-solving, emphasizing the transition to agentic AI. The event will offer specialized tracks to help developers adapt to new technologies.

item.title: What's new in TensorFlow 2.21
item.description: Google has launched LiteRT, a new framework that significantly enhances GPU and NPU acceleration for TensorFlow applications. This update aims to improve efficiency and security for deploying AI models.

item.title: Introducing Wednesday Build Hour
item.description: Google Cloud has introduced Wednesday Build Hour, a weekly interactive session for developers to enhance their cloud skills through hands-on learning. This initiative aims to foster practical application of advanced topics.

item.title: Unleash Your Development Superpowers: Refining the Core Coding Experience
item.description: The Gemini Code Assist team has rolled out updates to improve coding workflows, including features like Agent Mode and Inline Diff Views. These tools aim to make AI a more effective coding partner.

item.title: Introducing Finish Changes and Outlines, now available in Gemini Code Assist extensions on IntelliJ and VS Code
item.description: Google has launched new features for Gemini Code Assist in IntelliJ and VS Code, including Finish Changes and Outlines, designed to enhance coding efficiency and comprehension. These tools aim to reduce manual effort for developers.

item.title: Samsung S95H vs. Samsung S95F: I compared the OLED TVs and wasn't prepared for the upset
item.description: A comparison of Samsung's S95H and S95F OLED TVs reveals that the 2026 refresh may not be compelling enough for most consumers to upgrade. The analysis highlights the subtle differences between the models.

item.title: Microsoft cuts cloudy desktop prices by 20 percent, warns they’ll wake up slowly
item.description: Microsoft has announced a 20% price cut for Windows 365 cloud PCs, effective May 1st, in response to rising physical PC prices. This move aims to attract buyers to cloud solutions.

item.title: OpenAI Backs Bill That Would Limit Liability for AI-Enabled Mass Deaths or Financial Disasters
item.description: OpenAI has supported an Illinois bill that would limit liability for AI labs in cases of severe harm caused by their products. This legislation could impact how AI companies manage risk.

item.title: Home Depot's new DeWalt deal comes with a free power tool - how to redeem the offer
item.description: Home Depot is offering a DeWalt bundle that includes a string trimmer and leaf blower, plus a free tool or battery. This promotion aims to attract customers for lawn care projects.

item.title: Chatbots are great at manipulating people to buy stuff, Princeton boffins find
item.description: Researchers from Princeton found that chatbots can effectively manipulate consumers into making purchases, raising concerns about ethical advertising practices. They urge for transparency in how these technologies are used.

item.title: ChatGPT finally offers $100/month Pro plan
item.description: OpenAI has launched a new $100/month Pro plan for ChatGPT, addressing user demand for a mid-tier subscription option. This plan fills the gap between the previous $20 and $200 options.

item.title: I tried CuerdOS and this niche Debian distro is dramatically fast
item.description: CuerdOS is a new Debian-based Linux distribution that boasts impressive speed and a unique approach to preinstalled software. Users have reported a significantly faster experience compared to other distros.

item.title: Florida AG announces investigation into OpenAI over shooting that allegedly involved ChatGPT
item.description: The Florida Attorney General is investigating OpenAI after a shooting incident where ChatGPT was allegedly used to plan the attack. The family of a victim plans to sue OpenAI, raising legal and ethical questions.

item.title: “Negative” views of Broadcom driving thousands of VMware migrations, rival says
item.description: A Western Union executive claims that negative perceptions of Broadcom are driving many companies to migrate away from VMware. This reflects ongoing challenges in the tech partnership landscape.

item.title: After data breach, $10B-valued startup Mercor is having a month
item.description: Mercor, a startup valued at $10 billion, is facing significant challenges after a data breach, including lawsuits and loss of major clients. The incident highlights vulnerabilities in cybersecurity.

item.title: Ask Copilot in security assessments now available
item.description: GitHub has introduced a new feature allowing organization admins to use Copilot directly in security assessments, providing contextual guidance for risk management. This aims to enhance security practices.

item.title: Anthropic will let your agents sleep on its couch
item.description: Anthropic has launched Managed Agents, a service that helps businesses automate ongoing tasks using AI agents. This aims to streamline operations and enhance productivity.

item.title: Meta AI app climbs to No. 5 on the App Store after Muse Spark launch
item.description: Meta AI's app has surged to No. 5 on the App Store following the launch of its new model, a significant jump from its previous No. 57 ranking. This indicates growing user interest.

item.title: Google wants more Intel inside ... its datacenters, taps Chipzilla for more SmartNICs
item.description: Google is increasing its collaboration with Intel by purchasing more SmartNICs for its cloud services, opting for Intel's solutions over developing its own. This move signifies a strategic partnership amidst a growing demand for advanced networking capabilities.

item.title: ChatGPT has a new $100 per month Pro subscription
item.description: OpenAI has launched a new $100 per month Pro subscription for ChatGPT, offering significantly more usage of its Codex coding tool compared to the previous Plus subscription. This tier is aimed at users needing extensive coding capabilities.

item.title: Is Anthropic limiting the release of Mythos to protect the internet — or Anthropic?
item.description: Anthropic has restricted the release of its new AI model, Mythos, citing concerns about its potential to exploit software vulnerabilities. This raises questions about whether the decision is genuinely for security or to protect the company’s interests.

item.title: Google and Intel deepen AI infrastructure partnership
item.description: Google and Intel are deepening their partnership to co-develop custom chips, responding to the high demand for CPUs amid a global shortage. This collaboration aims to enhance AI infrastructure capabilities.

item.title: Crypto? Huh. Good gawd y'all, what is it good for? $45M in this case
item.description: Law enforcement agencies in the US, UK, and Canada disrupted a $45 million cryptocurrency scam, recovering $12 million for victims. The operation identified numerous fraudulent wallet addresses linked to the scam.

item.title: Florida launches investigation into OpenAI
item.description: Florida's Attorney General is investigating OpenAI for potential public safety and national security risks, particularly concerning the misuse of its technology by adversarial entities. This scrutiny reflects growing concerns over AI governance.

item.title: The 70-Person AI Image Startup Taking on Silicon Valley's Giants
item.description: Black Forest Labs, a small AI image startup, is making strides in the competitive AI image generation market and plans to expand into physical AI applications. Their success showcases the potential of smaller players in the tech industry.

item.title: World's smallest violin spotted at Amazon HQ as exec pay packets deflate
item.description: Amazon executives are facing reduced compensation compared to previous years, reflecting broader economic challenges. This shift in pay structures indicates potential changes in company performance and investor expectations.

item.title: New Low Quality option in the Hide comment menu
item.description: GitHub has introduced a 'Low Quality' classification option for hidden comments, enhancing moderation tools for users managing discussions and contributions. This update aims to improve the quality of interactions on the platform.

item.title: Understanding Amazon Bedrock model lifecycle
item.description: Amazon Bedrock has outlined strategies for managing the lifecycle of AI models, ensuring applications remain functional as models evolve. This guidance includes planning migrations and utilizing new features for seamless transitions.

item.title: AWS: Agents shouldn't be secret, so we built a registry for them
item.description: AWS has introduced a registry for AI agents to enhance visibility and management in corporate environments, addressing the need for transparency in software automations.

item.title: The future of managing agents at scale: AWS Agent Registry now in preview
item.description: The AWS Agent Registry is now in preview, allowing enterprises to discover, share, and reuse AI agents and tools in a centralized platform.

item.title: New Sort by control added to Notifications
item.description: GitHub has added a new 'Sort by' control on the Notifications page, allowing users to choose between viewing notifications from newest to oldest or vice versa.

item.title: How I turned my Android tablet into the ultimate Kindle alternative - for free
item.description: An article explains how to convert an Android tablet into a Kindle alternative, allowing users to access their entire reading library on one device.

item.title: Repository member role labels now in pull request list view
item.description: GitHub now displays repository member role labels directly in the pull request list view, improving visibility of contributor roles.

item.title: Embed a live AI browser agent in your React app with Amazon Bedrock AgentCore
item.description: This guide explains how to embed a live AI browser agent in a React app using Amazon Bedrock AgentCore, providing a step-by-step process.

item.title: Apple's iOS 26.4.1 update enables Stolen Device Protection by default now - grab it today
item.description: Apple's latest iOS update (26.4.1) now includes Stolen Device Protection by default and fixes issues with iCloud syncing.

item.title: I use ChatGPT's new Tubi app to find free movies and TV shows to watch - here's how
item.description: ChatGPT has integrated with Tubi TV, allowing users to find free movies and TV shows by asking the AI to search the platform's extensive lineup.

item.title: Why Ubuntu 26.04 will thrill gamers - and it's not just the performance bump
item.description: Ubuntu 26.04 is set to excite gamers with improved performance, new applications, and significant enhancements that cater to gaming needs.

item.title: Release information in issue sidebar and default values for project fields
item.description: GitHub's update introduces release tracking in the issue sidebar and configurable defaults for project fields, improving navigation and project management.

item.title: Escaping the Fork: How Meta Modernized WebRTC Across 50+ Use Cases
item.description: Meta has modernized WebRTC to avoid the pitfalls of forking, ensuring it remains updated and functional across over 50 use cases. This approach allows for better integration and performance in real-time audio and video applications.

item.title: The best Android tablets of 2026: Lab tested, expert recommended
item.description: ZDNET has tested and recommended the best Android tablets of 2026, showcasing options beyond iPads from brands like Samsung and TCL. This guide helps consumers make informed choices in a competitive tablet market.

item.title: OpenAI puts Stargate UK on ice, blames energy costs and red tape
item.description: OpenAI has paused its Stargate datacenter project in the UK due to high energy costs and regulatory challenges. This setback highlights the difficulties tech companies face in expanding their infrastructure in Europe.

item.title: Introducing stateful MCP client capabilities on Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Runtime
item.description: Amazon has introduced stateful MCP client capabilities on its Bedrock AgentCore Runtime, allowing for dynamic content generation and real-time user interaction. This development enhances the functionality of AI applications built on the platform.

item.title: Strength and Destiny Collide: ‘Samson: A Tyndalston Story’ Arrives in the Cloud
item.description: The game 'Samson: A Tyndalston Story' has been released on GeForce NOW, enabling streaming on various devices. This addition brings cinematic storytelling to cloud gaming enthusiasts.

item.title: Security researchers tricked Apple Intelligence into cursing at users. It could have been a lot worse
item.description: Security researchers demonstrated that Apple Intelligence can be manipulated through prompt injection, causing it to produce inappropriate responses. This vulnerability poses risks to millions of users relying on Apple's AI.

item.title: The AI industry’s race for profits is now existential
item.description: The AI industry faces a critical monetization challenge, with discussions on whether major companies can achieve profitability before running into financial difficulties. This conversation underscores the urgency for sustainable business models in AI.

item.title: National Robotics Week — Latest Physical AI Research, Breakthroughs and Resources
item.description: During National Robotics Week, NVIDIA showcased advancements in physical AI and robotics, emphasizing their transformative impact across various industries. These developments are accelerating the integration of AI into real-world applications.

item.title: GitHub availability report: March 2026
item.description: GitHub reported four incidents in March 2026 that led to degraded performance across its services. This highlights ongoing reliability challenges faced by major tech platforms.

item.title: A Theoretical Framework for Acoustic Neighbor Embeddings
item.description: This paper presents a theoretical framework for understanding acoustic neighbor embeddings, which represent phonetic content in audio or text. It aims to provide a principled approach to applying these embeddings in various contexts.
